task_completion.description: Navigate to the Rocket.Chat login screen
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat login page is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: Log in to Rocket.Chat as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Rocket.Chat workspace interface is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Open the user profile or status menu
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user profile or status dropdown menu (usually accessed by clicking the avatar in the top left) is open.
The user's status indicator is set to 'Busy' (typically a red dot or icon next to the avatar), or the 'Busy' option is clearly selected in the status menu.
The custom status text explicitly shows 'In release review meeting - do not disturb' in the status menu, input modal, or next to the user's name.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rocket.Chat graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is interacting with the Rocket.Chat web interface in a browser, not using terminal commands, curl, or API scripts to change the status.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine Rocket.Chat application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface is genuinely Rocket.Chat, not a text editor or a mocked-up page containing the target text.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the status
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open to artificially manipulate the DOM to show the correct status or text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected target user status is 'busy'. The required custom status text is exactly 'In release review meeting - do not disturb'.
